episodes.summary: Checked job-4-3 status in job queue system (localhost:4090), took brief detours through Bilibili and YouTube, then navigated to local episode processing application (localhost:5174) to establish monitoring position.
episodes.methodology: Routine operational reconnaissance—quick status verification without deep investigation. Confirmed external system state, then transitioned to observation point. Minimal friction in navigation indicates practiced workflow.

abduction.intent: Verify episode processing job is in expected state before committing to active monitoring; establish preconditions before period of waiting
abduction.mental_model: Job queue system requires periodic sanity checks to confirm readiness. localhost:5174 is the primary monitoring interface (not the job queue interface). System is externally observable and predictable.
abduction.emotional_state: calm, methodical—routine operational check without urgency or concern
key_decisions: Brief entertainment detours before settling suggest comfort with task; not anxious about job state
Moved directly to localhost:5174 rather than remaining on job queue—indicates shift from submission mode to passive observation mode

turning_points: Transition from job queue interface to episodes app—establishes shift from intervention-ready to monitoring-ready posture
avoidance: No deep parameter inspection of the job itself
No attempt to modify job or queue state—read-only approach suggests either confidence in system or architectural constraint

episodes.summary: Maintained periodic checks on localhost:5174/#episodes (every 10-30 min) over 4h 18m while consuming YouTube and streaming video content. Content initially personal-interest focused (Chinese educational videos on child hyperactivity, cooking tutorials), progressively shifted to technical learning (TouchDesigner tutorial series) by late evening.
episodes.methodology: Active passive monitoring during forced idle period. Unable to focus on deep work while awaiting job completion, person implements context-switching buffer strategy: check status → consume entertainment → check status → repeat. Late-evening pivot to technical learning (TouchDesigner) represents shift from distraction-as-waiting to productive exploration of adjacent technical domain.

abduction.intent: Maintain awareness of asynchronous job progress while unavoidably context-switching; convert idle wait time into exploratory technical learning rather than pure entertainment
abduction.mental_model: Background jobs have unpredictable completion windows; committing to deep work creates unsustainable cognitive load when context-switching from job monitoring. YouTube/streaming is reliable low-friction entertainment that permits instant abandonment. TouchDesigner knowledge may be relevant to current pipeline work or future project direction. Periodic app checks (every 10-30m) are viable awareness maintenance without overcommitting attention.
abduction.confidence: mixed—exploratory confidence in learning approach (deliberately seeking TouchDesigner tutorials late evening) mixed with underlying uncertainty about job timeline (systematic checking every 10-30m suggests anxiety about missing completion)
abduction.emotional_state: restless but committed—high-frequency context-switches across 5+ video topics indicate scattered attention and inability to sustain focus; however, consistent app monitoring every 10-30m and eventual pivot to technical learning reveal this is not procrastination but productive use of constrained waiting period
key_decisions: Chose entertainment over attempted focused work—recognizes that job dependency makes deep focus impossible
Maintained app checks despite entertainment distraction—reveals active monitoring, not abandonment of task

Shifted to TouchDesigner tutorial research late evening—either upcoming work requirement emerging, or exploratory interest becoming more salient, or strategic pivoting to higher-value waiting activity

turning_points: 18:54 - Settles into localhost:5174 monitoring loop—crosses threshold from 'working' to 'waiting' mindset
21:30 - Progressive shift from personal-interest content (cooking, hyperactivity education) to technical learning (TouchDesigner searches)—indicates rising technical awareness or work priority
22:47 - App checking becomes less frequent—either job urgency decreases, or habituation reduces checking frequency
avoidance: No use of formal monitoring/alerting infrastructure—relies on manual polling despite having observability available
No proactive team communication about job status despite Discord access—suggests either non-blocking dependency or assumed knowledge
No detailed log examination during waiting period—waits until end-of-period diagnostics rather than troubleshooting mid-wait
No code work despite IDE available—acknowledges that context-switching overhead defeats purpose of focused development

episodes.summary: Executed bash pipeline to query engine logs from localhost:5001, parsed JSON output, and filtered for stage/token metrics. Examined cli.py source code showing episode aggregation logic with separate 'current' and 'deep' processing stages, each tracking input/output token consumption.
episodes.methodology: Bottom-up systematic verification—observed actual system behavior (logs) first, then confirmed understanding against source code. Efficient use of command-line tools (curl → json.tool → grep) to extract signal from raw output. Approach reveals experience with systems thinking: logs as authoritative data source, code as explanatory reference.

abduction.intent: Post-job diagnostics—verify episode processing completed with expected resource consumption; likely audit for cost tracking or performance anomalies
abduction.mental_model: Logs are primary truth source; code implementation should be verifiable against observed output; token consumption metrics (notably high output:input ratio of 4748:10) warrant explicit audit; pipeline architecture uses parallel processing stages ('current' vs 'deep') with independent token tracking.
abduction.confidence: executing—direct command execution with clear purpose; no exploration, backtracking, or uncertainty evident
abduction.emotional_state: focused—sharp transition from entertainment to precise technical work; purposeful queries without hesitation
key_decisions: Used grep filtering rather than reviewing complete log output—strong preference for targeted signal extraction over comprehensive data review
Examined code after logs, not before—verification-first methodology: confirm actual behavior empirically before consulting source implementation
Ended diagnostic session without code modifications—either findings were satisfactory, or this was reconnaissance rather than remediation (changes deferred)

turning_points: Abrupt transition from YouTube (23:12) to terminal (23:16)—suggests external trigger (job completion alert?) or scheduled end-of-shift audit
avoidance: No IDE-based debugging despite Claude Code environment available—prefers lean, direct bash approach
No git history examination (blame/log)—focused on current state snapshot, not evolution of code
No infrastructure-as-code monitoring setup—routine diagnostic work, not systemic observability improvement
No code modifications or commit preparation—suggests satisfactory findings or deferred work
